All I Ching FAQ
Responsible Use

Can I use the I Ching for serious decisions?

Use the I Ching as a reflection and decision-review tool, not as a substitute for medical, legal, financial, safety, or professional advice.

DirectoryShort answerUse it as a decision reviewConcrete exampleWhen to step away from the tool

Short answer

The I Ching can be useful around serious decisions only if it stays in the right role. It may help you slow down, notice assumptions, name pressure, and review timing. It should not decide for you. It cannot verify facts, read another person's mind, diagnose health problems, replace a lawyer, calculate financial risk, or make an unsafe situation safe.

Use it as a decision review

For important choices, treat the reading as one reflective layer beside evidence, conversations, documents, and qualified advice. Read the primary hexagram as the current pattern, changing lines as pressure points, and the relating hexagram as a possible direction. Then translate the result into questions you can check in real life.

Concrete example

If you are considering signing a business contract and the reading points to limitation, the practical response is not to reject the deal automatically. A responsible response is to review the terms, ask what limit protects you, check payment timing, and speak with a qualified adviser if the amount is meaningful. The reading can improve attention, but it does not replace due diligence.

When to step away from the tool

Do not use the I Ching as the main guide for emergencies, health symptoms, legal disputes, large financial risks, safety threats, or crisis situations. In those cases, contact the appropriate professional or emergency service first. You can return to journaling later, after the immediate risk is handled.

Related reading
